  # Add brand picker step to signup flow                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Mon, 15 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Add a brand multi-select step after the existing fields in the signup form. The picker fetches brand options from   
  GET                                                                                                                 
  /auth/brands , allows multi-select and text search, and stores selections on the user via PATCH /auth/brands        
  immediately after the token is issued. No brand selection is valid — user can proceed with empty selection. Requires
  the backend sub-tasks to be merged first.                                                                           
                                                                                                                      
  ## Files to change                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                      
  • frontend/src/screens/Auth/Signup.jsx — add a brand picker step (searchable multi-select) after existing signup    
  fields                                                                                                              
  • frontend/src/api.js — add fetchBrands() (calls GET /auth/brands ) and updateBrands(brands) (calls PATCH           
  /auth/brands )                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] Signup includes a brand picker step: searchable multi-select populated from GET /auth/brands .                  
  [ ] Selected brands are stored on the user via PATCH /auth/brands after the token is issued.                        
  [ ] Empty selection is valid — user can proceed without selecting any brands.                                       
  [ ] npm run lint && npm run build passes in frontend/ .                                                             
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Linked Issues ------------------------                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  IS BLOCKED BY

  search by user brand preferences in find-pieces • Story • Medium • Idea

⭐ Story  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Mon, 15 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-29  💭 0 comments  🧵 2 linked                         
                                                                                                                      
  # Filter catalog search by user brand preferences in find-pieces                                                    
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Mon, 15 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  When the authenticated user has preferred_brands set (non-empty), add a SQL WHERE brand = ANY(...) filter in        
  searchByVibe . Empty selection = no filter (current behaviour preserved). Requires the user-prefs backend sub-task  
  to                                                                                                                  
  be merged first so preferred_brands is available.                                                                   
                                                                                                                      
  ## Files to change                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                      
  • backend/src/catalog/catalog-search.service.ts ( searchByVibe ) — accept optional brandFilter: string[] ; when non-
  empty, append AND brand = ANY($N::text[]) to the query                                                              
  • backend/test/unit/ — unit tests covering the filter branch                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] searchByVibe accepts an optional brandFilter: string[] parameter.                                               
  [ ] When non-empty, SQL query adds AND brand = ANY($N::text[]) .                                                    
  [ ] When empty or omitted, no brand filter is applied (existing behaviour).                                         
  [ ] Unit tests: with brands → only matching items returned; without brands → unfiltered.

⭐ Story  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Mon, 15 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-28  💭 0 comments  🧵 3 linked                         
                                                                                                                      
  # Add preferred_brands to User and expose via auth API                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Mon, 15 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Add preferred_brands String[] to the User Prisma model, expose it via GET /auth/me , add GET /auth/brands to return 
  brand options from the catalog, and add PATCH /auth/brands for updating selections.                                 
                                                                                                                      
  ## Files to change                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                      
  • backend/prisma/schema.prisma — add preferred_brands String[] (default [] ) to User                                
  • New Prisma migration                                                                                              
  • backend/src/auth/auth.dto.ts — add preferred_brands: string[] to UserProfileDto ; add request/response DTOs for   
  the brands endpoints                                                                                                
  • backend/src/auth/auth.service.ts — getMe returns preferred_brands ; listBrands queries SELECT DISTINCT brand FROM 
  catalog_items ORDER BY brand ; updateBrands updates the user's preferred_brands                                     
  • backend/src/auth/auth.controller.ts — add GET /auth/brands (no auth required) and PATCH /auth/brands              
  (JwtAuthGuard)                                                                                                      
  • backend/test/unit/ — unit tests                                                                                   
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] Migration adds preferred_brands String[] (default [] ) to users .                                               
  [ ] GET /auth/me response includes preferred_brands: string[] .                                                     
  [ ] GET /auth/brands returns distinct brand names from catalog_items.brand , sorted alphabetically.                 
  [ ] PATCH /auth/brands (JwtAuthGuard) accepts {{{ brands: string[] }}} and updates preferred_brands .               
  [ ] Unit tests: getMe returns the field; updateBrands persists the array; listBrands returns distinct values.

  # Wire onboarding routing in App.jsx and Settings replay entry                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Mon, 15 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Route first-time users ( user.onboarded_at === null ) to the Onboarding screen after signup, before the first real  
  screen. Returning users bypass it entirely. Add a "Replay tour" link in Settings. Requires KAN-16 (backend flag) and
  the Onboarding screen sub-task to be merged first.                                                                  
                                                                                                                      
  ## Files to change                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                      
  • frontend/src/App.jsx — on app mount, if user.onboarded_at === null , route to the Onboarding screen instead of the
  normal first screen                                                                                                 
  • frontend/src/screens/Settings/Settings.jsx — add a "Replay tour" entry that navigates to the Onboarding screen    
  regardless of onboarded_at                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] On app mount, if user.onboarded_at === null , the router sends the user to the Onboarding screen.               
  [ ] After "Got it" or "Skip" (either calls markOnboarded() ), the router moves to the first real screen             
  (VibeExtraction).                                                                                                   
  [ ] Returning users ( onboarded_at set) land directly on the normal flow — no tour.                                 
  [ ] Settings screen has a "Replay tour" entry that navigates to the Onboarding screen regardless of onboarded_at .  
  [ ] npm run lint && npm run build passes in frontend/ .

  ------------------------ Description ------------------------

  This sub-task is independent of the frontend tutorial format — it can be shipped before the UI is designed.h2. Files
  to change

  if currently null; idempotent (no-op if already set, timestamp unchanged)

  [ ] GET /auth/me response includes onboarded_at: Date | null .                                                      
  [ ] Newly created users have onboarded_at = null .                                                                  
  [ ] POST /auth/onboarded sets onboarded_at on the first call.                                                       
  [ ] Calling POST /auth/onboarded a second time leaves the original timestamp unchanged (idempotent).

⭐ Story  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Mon, 15 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-11  💭 0 comments  🧵 2 linked                         
                                                                                                                      
  # Log outfit compositions + saved/not-saved signal for model tuning                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Mon, 15 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  analytics, model-tuning, telemetry  👀 You are watching
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  ## Context                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  Today the only outfit signal that survives is the saves. If /style-pieces returns 3 outfits and the user saves 1,   
  the                                                                                                                 
  other 2 are gone the moment the user navigates away. Those rejections are the cleanest negative signal we have for  
  tuning the vibe → items → outfit chain (prompt edits, retrieval weights, why-string quality), and they're currently 
  invisible.                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  Note: outfit ids returned by style-pieces.service.ts ( outfit-1 , outfit-2 , ...) are call-local — Claude           
  regenerates                                                                                                         
  them per request. So we can't just "log the id"; we need server-side stable IDs.                                    
                                                                                                                      
  ## Scope                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                      
  Persist every outfit /style-pieces composes, give each one a stable UUID returned to the client, and flip a saved   
  flag when the user saves it via the wardrobe endpoint.                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ## Decisions (locked)                                                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  • **Server-side log at compose time** — every outfit Claude returns is a row in outfit_compositions . We know it was
  "seen" because the API returned it.                                                                                 
  • **Stable UUID returned in the**  /style-pieces  **response** — frontend treats it as the outfit's real id; passes 
  it through on save.                                                                                                 
  • **Saved-state is derived, not duplicated** — WardrobeOutfit.composition_id (FK, nullable) points back; "not saved"
  = no matching wardrobe row after N days. No separate boolean to keep in sync.                                       
  • **Snapshot the inputs alongside the output** — vibe anchor + aesthetic_tags + the item_ids that went into compose.
  Without these the log is useless for tuning.                                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ### Backend                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] Prisma migration adds:                                                                                          
      • OutfitComposition model: id (uuid pk) , user_id (uuid, fk, nullable for anonymous use) , vibe_anchor (string?) ,
      vibe_aesthetic_tags (string[]) , input_item_ids (string[]) , output_item_ids (string[]) , why (string?) ,       
      created_at .                                                                                                    
      • WardrobeOutfit.composition_id String? @db.Uuid + FK + index.                                                  
  [ ] StylePiecesService writes one OutfitComposition row per outfit returned and includes the new UUID in each       
  OutfitDto .                                                                                                         
  [ ] /style-pieces response shape: OutfitDto.id becomes the persisted UUID (replaces the ephemeral outfit-N ).       
  [ ] WardrobeService.saveLook accepts compositionId and persists it on WardrobeOutfit .                              
  [ ] Unit tests: composition rows written on every call; UUID round-trips into the wardrobe save; missing            
  compositionId is tolerated (legacy clients).                                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  ### Frontend                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] Results screen passes outfit.id (now a UUID) into the save call.                                                
  [ ] No UI changes otherwise — this is a logging story.                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ## Analytics value (why it's worth shipping)                                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  A simple query gives us:                                                                                            
                                                                                                                      
    SELECT vibe_anchor, COUNT(*) shown,                                                                               
           COUNT(wo.id) saved                                                                                         
    FROM outfit_compositions oc                                                                                       
    LEFT JOIN wardrobe_outfits wo ON wo.composition_id = oc.id                                                        
    GROUP BY vibe_anchor                                                                                              
    ORDER BY shown DESC;                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  → save rate per anchor, per aesthetic tag, per item, etc.                                                           
                                                                                                                      
  ## Out of scope (follow-ups)                                                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  • Frontend "impression beacon" for did-the-user-actually-scroll-to-it (compose-time logging already gives us a      
  useful 80%).                                                                                                        
  • Retention policy / TTL on the log table.                                                                          
  • Dashboard or admin UI on top of the data.                                                                         
  • Treating composition records as PII for GDPR (legal review separately if needed).                                 
                                                                                                                      
  ## Risks                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                      
  • Adds a write per outfit on the hot path. Should be a single bulk insert per /style-pieces call — cheap, but verify
  under load.                                                                                                         
  • If output_item_ids shape changes (e.g. weights, alternates), schema needs a small update — keep it as String[] for
  now.                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Linked Issues ------------------------                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  RELATES TO                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  KAN-8  Persist & display outfit "why" across the platform                • Story • Medium • In Progress KAN-10      
  Persist                                                                                                             
  vibe snapshot on saved outfits to preserve origin context • Story • Medium • Idea

⭐ Story  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Mon, 15 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-10  💭 0 comments  🧵 2 linked                         
                                                                                                                      
  # Persist vibe snapshot on saved outfits to preserve origin context                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Mon, 15 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  vibe-snapshot, wardrobe  👀 You are watching           
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  ## Context                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  WardrobeOutfit ( backend/prisma/schema.prisma ) currently keeps name , source (free-form string), extras , and      
  avatar_url — but no link back to the VibeProfile returned by /extract-vibe (anchor, aesthetic_tags, color_palette,  
  style_summary). Once an outfit is saved, the origin ("this was from a Carolyn Bessette vibe") only survives as      
  whatever string the user typed into source .                                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  This compounds with KAN-8 (saving the outfit why ): we end up storing the *result* (items + avatar) and the *reason*
  (why string) but not the *origin* (the vibe that produced it). That makes wardrobe filter/grouping by aesthetic     
  impossible without re-asking the user.                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ## Scope                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                      
  Persist a frozen snapshot of the vibe that produced each saved outfit, so the wardrobe can later be                 
  filtered/grouped/sorted by aesthetic.                                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  ## Decisions (locked)                                                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  • **Frozen snapshot** — copied at save time; never updated, never regenerated. Mirrors KAN-8's frozen- why decision.
  • **No backfill** — legacy WardrobeOutfit rows have vibe_snapshot = NULL ; UI handles null gracefully (no aesthetic 
  chip, no filter match).                                                                                             
  • **Whole-object snapshot, not FK** — store as Json , not a foreign key to a (nonexistent) vibes table. The vibe    
  /extract-vibe returns is ephemeral; we don't want to model it as a first-class entity yet.                          
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ### Backend                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] Prisma migration adds vibe_snapshot Json? to WardrobeOutfit ( backend/prisma/schema.prisma ).                   
  [ ] SaveLookDto and SaveWardrobeOutfitDto accept optional vibeSnapshot ( backend/src/wardrobe/wardrobe.dto.ts ),    
  shape matching VibeProfileResponseDto (anchor, aesthetic_tags, color_palette, style_summary).                       
  [ ] WardrobeService writes vibe_snapshot on create; field is immutable on update (the update DTO does NOT expose it 
  — same rule as KAN-8 why ).                                                                                         
  [ ] WardrobeOutfitResponseDto returns vibe_snapshot: VibeSnapshot | null .                                          
  [ ] Unit test: save with vibeSnapshot persists it; save without → null; update endpoint cannot mutate it.           
                                                                                                                      
  ### Frontend                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] Save-look call in frontend/src/api.js forwards the vibe from the /extract-vibe response alongside the outfit.   
  [ ] Wardrobe outfit detail ( frontend/src/screens/Wardrobe/ ) surfaces the anchor + a couple of aesthetic tags as   
  small chips. Null → render nothing (no empty container).                                                            
                                                                                                                      
  ## Out of scope (follow-ups)                                                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  • Wardrobe filter UI ("show me everything from a Bessette vibe") — needs design; file separately once we see the    
  data shape settle.                                                                                                  
  • Backfilling existing rows.                                                                                        
  • Promoting VibeProfile to a first-class table.                                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Linked Issues ------------------------                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  RELATES TO                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  KAN-8  Persist & display outfit "why" across the platform                • Story • Medium • In Progress KAN-11 Log  
  outfit compositions + saved/not-saved signal for model tuning • Story • Medium • Idea

⭐ Story  🚧 In Progress  ⌛ Mon, 15 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-8  💭 0 comments  🧵 2 linked                   
                                                                                                                      
  # Persist & display outfit "why" across the platform                                                                
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Mon, 15 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  outfit-why, results, wardrobe  👀 You are watching     
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  ## Context                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  /style-pieces ( backend/src/style-pieces/style-pieces.service.ts ) generates a one-sentence why per outfit (≤180    
  chars,                                                                                                              
  anchored to the vibe when present) and returns it in the response. The Discover results view (                      
  frontend/src/screens/Discover/Discover.jsx — ResultCard ) already renders why as a plain paragraph inline on each   
  look card, but the field is **dropped when a look is saved** — it is never forwarded to the backend, never persisted
  to the DB, and never shown in the saved Lookbook detail view ( frontend/src/screens/Looks/LookDetail.jsx ).         
                                                                                                                      
  ## Scope                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                      
  Persist why on saved outfits, restyle the existing Discover display to the locked quote-style treatment, and surface
  it in the Lookbook detail view.                                                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  ## Decisions (locked)                                                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  • **Frozen** — the why is an AI-generated artifact; users cannot edit it (unlike name ).                            
  • **No backfill / no regeneration** — legacy WardrobeOutfit rows without a why stay NULL ; UI hides the block when  
  null.                                                                                                               
  • **Display treatment** — small quote-style callout (italic, subtle left border or quote glyph), not a big banner.  
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ### Backend                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] Prisma migration adds why String? to WardrobeOutfit ( backend/prisma/schema.prisma ).                           
  [ ] SaveLookDto and SaveWardrobeOutfitDto accept optional why ( backend/src/wardrobe/wardrobe.dto.ts ).             
  [ ] WardrobeService writes why on outfit create; field is immutable on update ( UpdateWardrobeOutfitDto does NOT    
  expose it).                                                                                                         
  [ ] WardrobeOutfitResponseDto returns why: string | null .                                                          
  [ ] Unit test covers: save with why persists it; save without why → null; update endpoint cannot mutate why .       
                                                                                                                      
  ### Frontend                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] fromResultLook() ( frontend/src/utils/catalog-map.js ) includes why from the outfit in the payload passed to    
  saveLook .                                                                                                          
  [ ] saveLook() ( frontend/src/api.js ) sends why in the POST body to /wardrobe/looks .                              
  [ ] toLook() ( frontend/src/utils/catalog-map.js ) maps why from the backend response.                              
  [ ] ResultCard in frontend/src/screens/Discover/Discover.jsx re-styles the existing why paragraph as a quote-style  
  callout (italic, subtle left border or quote glyph).                                                                
  [ ] frontend/src/screens/Looks/LookDetail.jsx renders the same quote-style block below the outfit header.           
  [ ] When why is null or absent, no empty block is rendered in either view.                                          
                                                                                                                      
  ## Out of scope                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  • Backfilling existing outfits.                                                                                     
  • Letting users edit or regenerate the why .                                                                        
  • Showing why in the avatar prompt or share/export views.                                                           
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Linked Issues ------------------------                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  RELATES TO                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  KAN-10 Persist vibe snapshot on saved outfits to preserve origin context • Story • Medium • Idea KAN-11 Log outfit  
  compositions + saved/not-saved signal for model tuning • Story • Medium • Idea

⭐ Story  🚧 In Progress  ⌛ Mon, 15 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-7  💭 1 comments  🧵 0 linked                   
                                                                                                                      
  # Brand selection at signup & in settings, with brand filter on search                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Mon, 15 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  brand-preferences, onboarding, search  👀 You are      
  watching                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  ## Context                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  Users currently can't tell us which brands they actually buy or wear. Adding brand preferences lets us scope catalog
  retrieval (Stage 2 find-pieces) to brands a user is open to, improving relevance.                                   
                                                                                                                      
  ## Scope                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                      
  1. **Signup flow** — new step in registration to pick preferred brands (multi-select, searchable).                  
  2. **Settings screen** — edit the same brand preferences post-signup.                                               
  3. **Search filter** — find-pieces filters catalog_items by the user's selected brands.                             
                                                                                                                      
  ## Decisions (locked)                                                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  • **Brand list source** : derived at runtime from distinct brand values in catalog_items.brand — always in sync with
  ingested data.                                                                                                      
  • **Brand cap** : none; users may select as many brands as they wish.                                               
  • **Filter behaviour** : hard SQL filter — only return catalog items from selected brands. Empty selection = no     
  brand filter (current behaviour preserved).                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] User schema persists preferred_brands String[] (default [] ) — migration and Prisma model updated (             
  backend/prisma/schema.prisma ).                                                                                     
  [ ] GET /auth/brands returns distinct brand names from catalog_items.brand , sorted alphabetically, for the picker  
  UI.                                                                                                                 
  [ ] PATCH /auth/brands (JwtAuthGuard) accepts {{{ brands: string[] }}} and updates the user's preferred_brands .    
  [ ] GET /auth/me response includes preferred_brands: string[] .                                                     
  [ ] Registration screen ( frontend/src/screens/Auth/Signup.jsx ) has a brand picker step; submission stores         
  selections on the new user.                                                                                         
  [ ] Settings screen ( frontend/src/screens/Settings/Settings.jsx ) reads and writes the same field.                 
  [ ] POST /find-pieces applies the user's brand list as an additional SQL WHERE brand = ANY(...) filter in           
  backend/src/catalog/catalog-search.service.ts ( searchByVibe ).                                                     
  [ ] Empty selection = no brand filter (current behaviour preserved).                                                
  [ ] Unit tests cover the new brand filter branch in catalog-search.service .                                        
                                                                                                                      
  ## Out of scope                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  • Sorting or re-ranking by brand preference.                                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ 1 Comments ------------------------                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  Jae Yoon Bae • Mon, 15 Jun 26 • Latest comment                                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  1. it should be derived from the db the list of brands                                                              
  2. no cap for now                                                                                                   
  3. hard sql filter

Add server-side onboarding state to the User model and expose it via the auth API.

This sub-task is independent of the frontend tutorial format — it can be shipped before the UI is designed.

## Files to change

- `backend/prisma/schema.prisma` — add `onboarded_at DateTime?` to the User model
- New Prisma migration
- `backend/src/auth/auth.dto.ts` (or the `UserProfileDto`) — add `onboarded_at: Date | null` to the response shape
- `backend/src/auth/auth.service.ts` (`getMe`) — return `onboarded_at` from the user record
- `backend/src/auth/auth.controller.ts` — add `POST /auth/onboarded` (requires `JwtAuthGuard`): sets `onboarded_at = now()` if currently null; idempotent (no-op if already set, timestamp unchanged)
- `backend/test/unit/` — unit tests

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `GET /auth/me` response includes `onboarded_at: Date | null`.
- [ ] Newly created users have `onboarded_at = null`.
- [ ] `POST /auth/onboarded` sets `onboarded_at` on the first call.
- [ ] Calling `POST /auth/onboarded` a second time leaves the original timestamp unchanged (idempotent).
- [ ] Unit tests pass: unset→set transition; idempotency; `getMe` returns the field.

## Context

Users currently can't tell us which brands they actually buy or wear. Adding brand preferences lets us scope catalog retrieval (Stage 2 find-pieces) to brands a user is open to, improving relevance.

## Scope

1. **Signup flow** — new step in registration to pick preferred brands (multi-select, searchable).
2. **Settings screen** — edit the same brand preferences post-signup.
3. **Search filter** — find-pieces filters `catalog_items` by the user's selected brands.

## Decisions (locked)

- **Brand list source**: derived at runtime from distinct brand values in `catalog_items.brand` — always in sync with ingested data.
- **Brand cap**: none; users may select as many brands as they wish.
- **Filter behaviour**: hard SQL filter — only return catalog items from selected brands. Empty selection = no brand filter (current behaviour preserved).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] User schema persists `preferred_brands String[]` (default `[]`) — migration and Prisma model updated (`backend/prisma/schema.prisma`).
- [ ] `GET /auth/brands` returns distinct brand names from `catalog_items.brand`, sorted alphabetically, for the picker UI.
- [ ] `PATCH /auth/brands` (JwtAuthGuard) accepts `{ brands: string[] }` and updates the user's `preferred_brands`.
- [ ] `GET /auth/me` response includes `preferred_brands: string[]`.
- [ ] Registration screen (`frontend/src/screens/Auth/Signup.jsx`) has a brand picker step; submission stores selections on the new user.
- [ ] Settings screen (`frontend/src/screens/Settings/Settings.jsx`) reads and writes the same field.
- [ ] `POST /find-pieces` applies the user's brand list as an additional SQL `WHERE brand = ANY(...)` filter in `backend/src/catalog/catalog-search.service.ts` (`searchByVibe`).
- [ ] Empty selection = no brand filter (current behaviour preserved).
- [ ] Unit tests cover the new brand filter branch in `catalog-search.service`.

## Out of scope

- Sorting or re-ranking by brand preference.

## Needs human input

- [ ] This story covers four sub-concerns (User model + auth API, catalog filter, signup UI, settings UI) and is not atomic as written. KAN-28, KAN-29, KAN-30, and KAN-31 already address these sub-scopes as standalone Stories. Should those be formally linked as sub-tasks of this parent, or should this parent be closed/archived once all four child stories complete?

## Context

`/style-pieces` (`backend/src/style-pieces/style-pieces.service.ts`) generates a one-sentence `why` per outfit (≤180 chars, anchored to the vibe when present) and returns it in the response. The Discover results view (`frontend/src/screens/Discover/Discover.jsx` — `ResultCard`) already renders `why` as a plain paragraph inline on each look card, but the field is **dropped when a look is saved** — it is never forwarded to the backend, never persisted to the DB, and never shown in the saved Lookbook detail view (`frontend/src/screens/Looks/LookDetail.jsx`).

## Scope

Persist `why` on saved outfits, restyle the existing Discover display to the locked quote-style treatment, and surface it in the Lookbook detail view.

## Decisions (locked)

- **Frozen** — the `why` is an AI-generated artifact; users cannot edit it (unlike `name`).
- **No backfill / no regeneration** — legacy `WardrobeOutfit` rows without a `why` stay NULL; UI hides the block when null.
- **Display treatment** — small quote-style callout (italic, subtle left border or quote glyph), not a big banner.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Backend

- [ ] Prisma migration adds `why String?` to `WardrobeOutfit` (`backend/prisma/schema.prisma`).
- [ ] `SaveLookDto` and `SaveWardrobeOutfitDto` accept optional `why` (`backend/src/wardrobe/wardrobe.dto.ts`).
- [ ] `WardrobeService` writes `why` on outfit create; field is immutable on update (`UpdateWardrobeOutfitDto` does NOT expose it).
- [ ] `WardrobeOutfitResponseDto` returns `why: string | null`.
- [ ] Unit test covers: save with `why` persists it; save without `why` → null; update endpoint cannot mutate `why`.

### Frontend

- [ ] `fromResultLook()` (`frontend/src/utils/catalog-map.js`) includes `why` from the outfit in the payload passed to `saveLook`.
- [ ] `saveLook()` (`frontend/src/api.js`) sends `why` in the POST body to `/wardrobe/looks`.
- [ ] `toLook()` (`frontend/src/utils/catalog-map.js`) maps `why` from the backend response.
- [ ] `ResultCard` in `frontend/src/screens/Discover/Discover.jsx` re-styles the existing `why` paragraph as a quote-style callout (italic, subtle left border or quote glyph).
- [ ] `frontend/src/screens/Looks/LookDetail.jsx` renders the same quote-style block below the outfit header.
- [ ] When `why` is null or absent, no empty block is rendered in either view.

## Out of scope

- Backfilling existing outfits.
- Letting users edit or regenerate the `why`.
- Showing `why` in the avatar prompt or share/export views.

## Needs human input

- [ ] This story covers two concerns (backend persistence and frontend display) and is not atomic as written. Two separate implementation chains appear to cover identical scope: KAN-14 (backend) + KAN-15 (frontend) and KAN-18 (backend) + KAN-19 (frontend). Both chains touch the same files with the same acceptance criteria. Which pair is the intended implementation path? The other pair should be closed to avoid conflicting work.
- [ ] Should the chosen implementation pair be formally linked as sub-tasks of this parent story?

## Context

`WardrobeOutfit` (`backend/prisma/schema.prisma`) currently keeps `name`, `source` (free-form string), `extras`, and `avatar_url` — but no link back to the `VibeProfile` returned by `/extract-vibe` (`anchor`, `aesthetic_tags`, `color_palette`, `style_summary`). Once an outfit is saved, the origin ("this was from a Carolyn Bessette vibe") only survives as whatever string the user typed into `source`.

This compounds with KAN-8 (saving the outfit `why`): we end up storing the result (items + avatar) and the reason (`why` string) but not the origin (the vibe that produced it). That makes wardrobe filter/grouping by aesthetic impossible without re-asking the user.

## Scope

Persist a frozen snapshot of the vibe that produced each saved outfit, so the wardrobe can later be filtered/grouped/sorted by aesthetic.

## Decisions (locked)

- **Frozen snapshot** — copied at save time; never updated, never regenerated. Mirrors KAN-8's frozen-`why` decision.
- **No backfill** — legacy `WardrobeOutfit` rows have `vibe_snapshot = NULL`; UI handles null gracefully (no aesthetic chip, no filter match).
- **Whole-object snapshot, not FK** — store as `Json`, not a foreign key to a (nonexistent) vibes table. The vibe `/extract-vibe` returns is ephemeral; we don't want to model it as a first-class entity yet.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Backend

- [ ] Prisma migration adds `vibe_snapshot Json?` to `WardrobeOutfit` (`backend/prisma/schema.prisma`).
- [ ] `SaveLookDto` and `SaveWardrobeOutfitDto` accept optional `vibeSnapshot` (`backend/src/wardrobe/wardrobe.dto.ts`), shape matching `VibeProfileResponseDto` (`anchor`, `aesthetic_tags`, `color_palette`, `style_summary`).
- [ ] `WardrobeService` writes `vibe_snapshot` on create; field is immutable on update (the update DTO does NOT expose it — same rule as KAN-8 `why`).
- [ ] `WardrobeOutfitResponseDto` returns `vibe_snapshot: VibeSnapshot | null`.
- [ ] Unit test: save with `vibeSnapshot` persists it; save without → null; update endpoint cannot mutate it.

### Frontend

- [ ] Save-look call in `frontend/src/api.js` forwards the vibe from the `/extract-vibe` response alongside the outfit.
- [ ] Wardrobe outfit detail (`frontend/src/screens/Wardrobe/`) surfaces the `anchor` + a couple of `aesthetic_tags` as small chips. Null → render nothing (no empty container).

## Out of scope

- Wardrobe filter UI ("show me everything from a Bessette vibe") — needs design; file separately once we see the data shape settle.
- Backfilling existing rows.
- Promoting `VibeProfile` to a first-class table.

## Needs human input

- [ ] This story covers two concerns (backend schema/service and frontend display) and is not atomic as written. KAN-20 (add `vibe_snapshot` to `WardrobeOutfit` and expose via wardrobe API) and KAN-21 (forward vibe through the save call and surface anchor and tags in Wardrobe detail) already cover these sub-scopes as standalone Stories. Should those be formally linked as sub-tasks of this parent, or should this parent be closed/archived once both child stories complete?

input.content:
## Context

Today the only outfit signal that survives is the saves. If `/style-pieces` returns 3 outfits and the user saves 1, the other 2 are gone the moment the user navigates away. Those rejections are the cleanest negative signal we have for tuning the vibe → items → outfit chain (prompt edits, retrieval weights, `why`-string quality), and they're currently invisible.

Note: outfit ids returned by `style-pieces.service.ts` (`outfit-1`, `outfit-2`, ...) are call-local — Claude regenerates them per request. So we can't just "log the id"; we need server-side stable IDs.

## Scope

Persist every outfit `/style-pieces` composes, give each one a stable UUID returned to the client, and flip a saved flag when the user saves it via the wardrobe endpoint.

## Decisions (locked)

- **Server-side log at compose time** — every outfit Claude returns is a row in `outfit_compositions`. We know it was "seen" because the API returned it.
- **Stable UUID returned in the `/style-pieces` response** — frontend treats it as the outfit's real id; passes it through on save.
- **Saved-state is derived, not duplicated** — `WardrobeOutfit.composition_id` (FK, nullable) points back; "not saved" = no matching wardrobe row after N days. No separate boolean to keep in sync.
- **Snapshot the inputs alongside the output** — vibe anchor + `aesthetic_tags` + the `item_ids` that went into compose. Without these the log is useless for tuning.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Backend

- [ ] Prisma migration adds: `OutfitComposition` model (`id` uuid PK, `user_id` uuid FK nullable, `vibe_anchor` string?, `vibe_aesthetic_tags` string[], `input_item_ids` string[], `output_item_ids` string[], `why` string?, `created_at`); `WardrobeOutfit.composition_id String? @db.Uuid` + FK + index.
- [ ] `StylePiecesService` writes one `OutfitComposition` row per outfit returned and includes the new UUID in each `OutfitDto`.

## Context

Today the only outfit signal that survives is the saves. If `/style-pieces` returns 3 outfits and the user saves 1, the other 2 are gone the moment the user navigates away. Those rejections are the cleanest negative signal we have for tuning the vibe → items → outfit chain (prompt edits, retrieval weights, `why`-string quality), and they're currently invisible.

Note: outfit ids returned by `style-pieces.service.ts` (`outfit-1`, `outfit-2`, ...) are call-local — Claude regenerates them per request. So we can't just "log the id"; we need server-side stable IDs.

## Scope

Persist every outfit `/style-pieces` composes, give each one a stable UUID returned to the client, and flip a saved flag when the user saves it via the wardrobe endpoint.

## Decisions (locked)

- **Server-side log at compose time** — every outfit Claude returns is a row in `outfit_compositions`. We know it was "seen" because the API returned it.
- **Stable UUID returned in the `/style-pieces` response** — frontend treats it as the outfit's real id; passes it through on save.
- **Saved-state is derived, not duplicated** — `WardrobeOutfit.composition_id` (FK, nullable) points back; "not saved" = no matching wardrobe row after N days. No separate boolean to keep in sync.
- **Snapshot the inputs alongside the output** — vibe anchor + `aesthetic_tags` + the `item_ids` that went into compose. Without these the log is useless for tuning.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Backend

- [ ] Prisma migration adds: `OutfitComposition` model (`id` uuid PK, `user_id` uuid FK nullable, `vibe_anchor` string?, `vibe_aesthetic_tags` string[], `input_item_ids` string[], `output_item_ids` string[], `why` string?, `created_at`); `WardrobeOutfit.composition_id String? @db.Uuid` + FK + index.
- [ ] `StylePiecesService` writes one `OutfitComposition` row per outfit returned and includes the new UUID in each `OutfitDto`.
- [ ] `/style-pieces` response shape: `OutfitDto.id` becomes the persisted UUID (replaces the ephemeral `outfit-N`).
- [ ] `WardrobeService.saveLook` accepts `compositionId` and persists it on `WardrobeOutfit`.
- [ ] Unit tests: composition rows written on every call; UUID round-trips into the wardrobe save; missing `compositionId` is tolerated (legacy clients).

### Frontend

- [ ] Results screen passes `outfit.id` (now a UUID) into the save call.
- [ ] No UI changes otherwise — this is a logging story.

## Analytics value

A simple query gives us save rate per anchor, per aesthetic tag, per item, etc.:

```sql
SELECT vibe_anchor, COUNT(*) shown, COUNT(wo.id) saved
FROM outfit_compositions oc
LEFT JOIN wardrobe_outfits wo ON wo.composition_id = oc.id
GROUP BY vibe_anchor
ORDER BY shown DESC;
```

## Out of scope

- Frontend "impression beacon" for scroll visibility.
- Retention policy / TTL on the log table.
- Dashboard or admin UI on top of the data.
- GDPR review of composition records.

## Risks

- Adds a write per outfit on the hot path. Should be a single bulk insert per `/style-pieces` call — cheap, but verify under load.
- If `output_item_ids` shape changes (e.g. weights, alternates), schema needs a small update — keep it as `String[]` for now.
